players.name: Nawaf Al-Aqidi
players.bio: Young shot-stopper at Al-Nassr who has quickly established himself as a contender between the posts for the Green Falcons. At just 26, this is shaping up to be his breakthrough tournament on the world stage.
players.facts: Born on 10 May 2000, Al-Aqidi is literally a 21st-century goalkeeper — a millennial between the sticks.

players.name: Ali Majrashi
players.bio: Solid and dependable, Ali Majrashi brings composure and defensive nous from Al-Ahli to Saudi Arabia's backline. With 21 caps at 26, he's earning his stripes as a trusted member of the Green Falcons' defence.
players.facts: Majrashi plays for Al-Ahli — one of the oldest football clubs in Saudi Arabia, founded back in 1947.

players.name: Ali Lajami
players.bio: A composed left-sided defender with Al-Hilal pedigree, Ali Lajami brings experience and the occasional goal threat to the Saudi backline. His lone international goal is a treasured memory for Green Falcons fans.
players.facts: Lajami has scored exactly 1 goal in 24 caps — a conversion rate that won't trouble Ronaldo, but he'll take it.

players.name: Abdulelah Al-Amri
players.bio: One of Saudi Arabia's more experienced defenders with 44 caps, Abdulelah Al-Amri is a reliable presence at the back for the Green Falcons. The Al-Nassr man brings club form and international steadiness to WC 2026.
players.facts: Al-Amri shares his Al-Nassr club with Cristiano Ronaldo — though their training ground conversations are presumably in different languages.

players.name: Hassan Al-Tambakti
players.bio: A commanding centre-back with 54 caps, Hassan Al-Tambakti is the defensive cornerstone of the Green Falcons. Powerful in the air and composed on the ball, the Al-Hilal stalwart is one of Asia's finest defenders.
players.facts: Al-Tambakti has more Saudi Arabia caps (54) than any other defender in this squad — the undisputed leader of the back four.

players.name: Nasser Al-Dawsari
players.bio: A creative and industrious midfielder for Al-Hilal, Nasser Al-Dawsari provides energy and vision in the engine room of the Green Falcons. Still only 27, he's maturing into a key figure for Saudi Arabia's midfield.
players.facts: Not to be confused with his more famous compatriot Salem Al-Dawsari — sharing a surname in Saudi football is almost a prerequisite.

players.name: Musab Al-Juwayr
players.full_name: Musab Fahd Al-Juwayr; مُصْعَب فَهْد الْجُوَيْر
players.bio: Born in 2003, Musab Al-Juwayr is one of the tournament's most exciting young talents. The Al-Qadsiah midfielder has already racked up 6 international goals in 37 caps — remarkable numbers for someone who can't yet rent a car in the US.
players.facts: Al-Juwayr was only 19 when he made his international debut — and he's already outscored several teammates twice his age.

players.name: Ayman Yahya
players.full_name: Ayman Yahya Salem Ahmed; أَيْمَن يَحْيَى سَالِم أَحْمَد
players.bio: A pacy and tricky left winger from Riyadh, Ayman Yahya brings directness and flair to Saudi Arabia's attack. The Al-Nassr wideman is still developing his international game but has the tools to cause defences real problems.
players.facts: Yahya plays his club football alongside Cristiano Ronaldo at Al-Nassr — imagine the tips you'd pick up in training.

players.name: Firas Al-Buraikan
players.full_name: Firas bin Tariq bin Nasser Al-Buraikan; فراس البريكان
players.bio: Saudi Arabia's sharpest striker, Firas Al-Buraikan has 16 goals in 72 caps — a record that speaks for itself. The Al-Ahli forward is direct, clinical, and the man the Green Falcons look to when they need a goal.
players.facts: Al-Buraikan shares his birthday — 14 May — with teammate Ayman Yahya. Two Green Falcons, one birthday cake.

players.name: Salem Al-Dawsari
players.full_name: Salem bin Mohammed bin Shafi Al-Dawsari; سالم محمد الدوسري
players.bio: A Saudi legend. Salem Al-Dawsari's 27 goals in 111 caps include THAT stunning winner against Argentina at Qatar 2022. The Al-Hilal winger is the heartbeat of the Green Falcons and arrives at WC 2026 with something to prove.
players.facts: His goal against Argentina in 2022 is arguably the greatest World Cup upset strike of the century. Messi was not amused.

players.name: Saleh Al-Shehri
players.full_name: Saleh Khalid Mohammed Al-Shehri; صالح بن خالد بن محمد الشهري
players.bio: A seasoned striker with 17 international goals, Saleh Al-Shehri is a proven finisher who complements Saudi Arabia's attacking options. The Al-Ittihad forward brings experience, physicality, and a sharp eye for goal.
players.facts: Al-Shehri scored Saudi Arabia's equaliser against Argentina in Qatar 2022 — the goal that started one of football's greatest shocks.

players.name: Saud Abdulhamid
players.full_name: Saud Abdullah Salem Abdulhamid; سعود عبدالحميد
players.bio: Saudi Arabia's most exported talent, Saud Abdulhamid plies his trade at Lens in France's Ligue 1 — a rarity for a Saudi player. His European experience makes him one of the Green Falcons' most tactically refined defenders.
players.facts: Abdulhamid is one of very few Saudi players to play top-flight European football — a genuine trailblazer for Saudi football.

players.name: Nawaf Boushal
players.full_name: Nawaf Mashari Abdulrahman Boushal; نواف مشاري بوشل
players.bio: A dependable right-back from Al-Hasa, Nawaf Boushal provides solid defensive cover and overlapping energy for the Green Falcons. With 27 caps and still only 26, he's becoming a reliable fixture in the Saudi setup.
players.facts: Boushal hails from Al-Hasa — home to one of the world's largest date palm oases. Grounded in every sense of the word.

players.name: Hassan Kadesh
players.full_name: Hassan Kadesh Mahboob; حَسَن كَادِش مَحْبُوب
players.bio: A Dammam-born left-back with two international goals to his name, Hassan Kadesh offers attacking intent from deep. The Al-Ittihad defender is one of the elder statesmen of this Saudi squad at 33.
players.facts: Kadesh has scored 2 goals as a left-back — more than some of Saudi Arabia's dedicated forwards. Priorities questioned, results noted.

players.name: Abdullah Al-Khaibari
players.full_name: Abdullah Mohammed Al-Khaibari; عبد الله محمد الخيبري
players.bio: The anchor of Saudi Arabia's midfield, Abdullah Al-Khaibari shields the defence with intelligence and discipline. The Al-Nassr man's 42 caps reflect his importance as the unsung engine that keeps the Green Falcons ticking.
players.facts: Al-Khaibari has 42 caps and zero goals — the classic defensive midfielder who considers an accurate sideways pass a work of art.

players.name: Ziyad Al-Johani
players.full_name: Ziyad Mubarak Eid Al-Marwani Al-Johani; زياد مبارك المرواني الجهني
players.bio: Born on 11/11/2001, Ziyad Al-Johani is one of the squad's younger midfield options, bringing fresh legs and competitive instincts from Al-Ahli. With just 12 caps, WC 2026 could be his coming-out party on the world stage.
players.facts: Al-Johani was born on 11/11 — Remembrance Day, Armistice Day, and apparently also a great day for defensive midfielders.

players.name: Khalid Al-Ghannam
players.bio: Khalid Al-Ghannam is the wild card of this Saudi squad — only 7 caps but enough raw talent to earn a World Cup spot. The Al-Ettifaq winger from Khobar offers pace and directness from the left flank.
players.facts: With just 7 caps before a World Cup, Al-Ghannam is one of the least experienced players in the tournament. No pressure, then.

players.name: Alaa Al-Hejji
players.bio: A late bloomer on the international stage, Alaa Al-Hejji arrives at WC 2026 with just 3 caps to his name. The Neom midfielder is a squad player whose club form earned him this remarkable opportunity at 30.
players.facts: Al-Hejji plays for Neom FC — a club backed by Saudi Arabia's futuristic $500 billion megacity project. Very on-brand.

players.name: Abdullah Al-Hamdan
players.bio: A clinical finisher with 13 goals in 52 caps, Abdullah Al-Hamdan is one of Saudi Arabia's most dangerous attacking weapons. The Al-Nassr forward combines intelligent movement with a composed finish in front of goal.
players.facts: Al-Hamdan has scored 13 goals in 52 caps — a better international strike rate than many recognised Saudi strikers before him.

players.name: Sultan Mandash
players.bio: A versatile forward with 2 goals in just 7 caps, Sultan Mandash brings experience and finishing quality from Al-Hilal. At 31, this World Cup could be the veteran's final shot at the grandest stage of all.
players.facts: Mandash has 2 goals in 7 caps — a 100% better goals-per-game rate than some teammates with ten times the appearances.

players.name: Mohammed Al-Owais
players.bio: The most experienced goalkeeper in the squad, Mohammed Al-Owais has been the Green Falcons' number one for years. His 65 caps include the historic Qatar 2022 campaign where he made crucial saves against Argentina.
players.facts: Al-Owais was Saudi Arabia's hero vs Argentina in 2022, making multiple stunning saves. He kept Messi quiet. Briefly, but still.

players.name: Ahmed Al-Kassar
players.bio: A veteran third-choice goalkeeper, Ahmed Al-Kassar brings experience and professionalism to the squad at 35. With 9 caps across his career, the Al-Qadsiah stopper knows his role — and fulfils it with distinction.
players.facts: At 35, Al-Kassar is the oldest goalkeeper in the Saudi squad and is attending his World Cup as a seasoned professional's finale.

players.name: Mohamed Kanno
players.bio: A combative and technically gifted midfielder, Mohamed Kanno is the engine room of the Green Falcons. With 79 caps and 8 goals, the Al-Hilal man brings leadership, bite, and creativity — often in the same challenge.
players.facts: Kanno has 79 caps for Saudi Arabia — making him one of the most experienced players in this entire squad. A genuine talisman.

players.name: Moteb Al-Harbi
players.bio: A young and promising centre-back at Al-Hilal, Moteb Al-Harbi has earned 13 caps with performances suggesting there are many more to come. Physically imposing and positionally aware, he's one for the future — and the present.
players.facts: Al-Harbi was born on 20 February 2000 — a true millennial defender who has grown up watching the Green Falcons he now represents.

players.name: Jehad Thakri
players.bio: One of the squad's youngest defenders at 24, Jehad Thakri has made his mark with 8 caps from Al-Qadsiah. Combative and determined, he's fighting for a starting role at a World Cup that could define his career.
players.facts: Thakri is only 24 and already at a World Cup — Al-Qadsiah's academy system deserves enormous credit for his development.

players.name: Mohammed Abu Al-Shamat
players.bio: At just 23, Mohammed Abu Al-Shamat is the youngest defender in the Saudi squad and one of the youngest players at WC 2026. The Al-Qadsiah man's 8 caps hint at a player being carefully cultivated for future stardom.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Abu Al-Shamat was only 3 years old when Saudi Arabia last qualified for a World Cup in 2006. Time flies.
